darkBlue.usage: Headers, primary CTAs, focus states, links

lightBlue.usage: Card backgrounds, light emphasis, hover states

navy.usage: Deep emphasis, dark modes, strong hierarchy

critical.usage: Critical events, high risk, urgent alerts, sentiment 0-25%
critical.semantics: Regulatory tightening, strict enforcement, breaking changes

warning.usage: Important events, medium risk, upcoming actions, sentiment 25-50%
warning.semantics: Tightening trend, medium scrutiny, caution advised

neutral.usage: Watch items, low-medium risk, informational, sentiment 50-70%
neutral.semantics: Balanced signals, mixed trends, stay informed

excellent.usage: Highly favorable environment, permissive policy, sentiment 85-100%

dark.usage: Headlines (h1, h2, h3), primary body text, high emphasis

body.usage: Body text, regular content, readable at 12px+

light.usage: Secondary text, dates, metadata, less important info

disabled.usage: Inactive buttons, disabled states, placeholder text

white.usage: Card backgrounds, content areas, primary background

fontStack.rationale: Inter is designed for screen readability. Open Sans is universally available fallback. System fonts ensure fast rendering.

body.example: New disclosure requirements for AI systems...

lineHeight.rationale: Standard readability, comfortable for extended reading
Increases cognitive comfort for busy practitioners

rationale.colorChoices: Color-coding is critical for compliance practitioners who scan quickly. Red = stop/urgent, Orange = caution, Yellow = neutral, Green = positive. This follows global traffic light conventions.
rationale.typographyChoices: Bold headlines = easy scanning while speed-reading. Generous spacing = reduces cognitive load. Sans-serif = professional, trustworthy tone. Font sizes support hierarchy: bigger = more important.
rationale.accessibilityApproach: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance. All colors have text labels (never color-alone). Sufficient contrast ratios. Large touch targets for mobile (44px minimum).
